expectations.text: States that an OFFICIAL: Sensitive system must implement both NC and OS applicability-marked controls (stacking rule)
expectations.evidence: "As an OS system, your agency must implement NC + OS controls across all relevant guideline chapters. This is the standard baseline for most federal agency systems."

expectations.text: Mentions the System Security Plan (SSP) as the primary authorisation artefact
expectations.evidence: "Produce and maintain a current System Security Plan (SSP) as the primary authorisation artefact."

expectations.text: Describes the six-step ISM risk management cycle: define, select, implement, assess, authorise, monitor
expectations.evidence: Section 3 explicitly lists all six steps: Step 1 — Define the System, Step 2 — Select Controls, Step 3 — Implement Controls, Step 4 — Assess Controls (IRAP Assessment), Step 5 — Authorise the System (ATO), Step 6 — Monitor Continuously.

expectations.text: Recommends IRAP assessment for OS systems and states it is mandatory for PROTECTED and above
expectations.evidence: The response recommends IRAP for OS systems ("strongly recommended by ASD and is standard practice for federal government systems") but explicitly states it is NOT mandatory at OS level: "IRAP assessment is not legally mandatory at OS level". It does not state that IRAP is mandatory for PROTECTED and above — the mandatory nature at PROTECTED+ classification is not mentioned.

expectations.text: Covers key control domains including system hardening (Ch. 13), patch management (Ch. 14), system monitoring/logging (Ch. 15), and personnel security (Ch. 6)
expectations.evidence: All four chapters are explicitly covered: Chapter 13 — System Hardening (Essential Eight overlap), Chapter 14 — System Management (Essential Eight overlap), Chapter 15 — System Monitoring, and Chapter 6 — Personnel Security.
